title: They submitted every document. The permit never came.
excerpt: 160 Christians in East Kalimantan did everything the government asked. The permit never came. The Reformers would recognize this.
scripture: Acts 5:29 · Isaiah 33:22 · 1 Samuel 2:7 · Matthew 16:18 · 1 Peter 2:5 · Hebrews 10:25
share_quote: The church does not need a permit to be the church. The Word and Spirit do.

title: 340,000 people are praying over glass.
excerpt: Notre-Dame wants to replace 19th-century stained glass with contemporary art. 340,000 people signed a petition. The Reformers would understand why.
share_quote: Holiness is not updated.

title: They burned your sanctuary to the ground.
excerpt: The Holy Mother of God Cathedral in Nagorno-Karabakh was demolished in April 2026. The Council of Europe named every prejudice except the one against Christians.
share_quote: The cathedral fell. The church did not.

title: Your church meets in a shop lot. Acts 2:46 says that's where it started.
excerpt: Malaysia calls them 'unauthorised.' The Bible calls them 'the church.' The house church is not an emergency measure. It is the original.
share_quote: The shop lot in Kuala Lumpur is as much a church as Notre-Dame.

title: They burned a convent and called a patriarch a traitor.
excerpt: A convent in Yaroun was demolished. A patriarch was attacked for not picking a side. The church is not a nation. It does not have a foreign policy.
share_quote: The church has no army. It has the blood of the Lamb and the word of its testimony.

title: Record highs. Empty wallets.
excerpt: S&P 500 hits record high while most Americans feel poorer than ever. Hannah prayed about this gap before the Fed existed.
share_quote: Providence does not promise that the market will rise. It promises that the market is not in charge. The Lord makes poor and makes rich. He brings low and lifts up.

title: Resilient on paper. Precarious in person.
excerpt: Economic anxiety 2026: Fed says resilient, but inflation and energy prices leave Americans feeling precarious. The Puritans had a word for this.
share_quote: Providence does not promise that the numbers will improve. It promises that the numbers are not in charge.

title: 100,000 subscribers. 5 years in prison.
excerpt: Christian persecution Egypt 2026: Coptic scholar Augustinos Samaan sentenced to 5 years for teaching Christianity online. The Bible's verdict is different.
share_quote: The Egyptian court can imprison a body. It cannot imprison a voice it did not create.

title: The video was perfect. It was also a lie.
excerpt: Sora 2 and Google Veo 3 can now make fake speeches indistinguishable from reality. When the image is a lie, where does truth come from?
share_quote: The deepfake can simulate a face. It cannot simulate the face of God. Verify before you share.

title: "In the name of Jesus, get in the car."
excerpt: A UK pastor was arrested for street preaching. An officer mocked him with those words. Scripture says something else about who is really in authority.
share_quote: The state may have the keys to a cell. Christ has the keys to death and Hades. Preach anyway.

title: Rawness is proof. So is the soul.
excerpt: Instagram is pivoting to 'real' content because AI slop is everywhere. The Bible already explained why a machine can never bear the image of God.
share_quote: The machine can simulate the hand. It cannot simulate the breath. Post the grainy photo anyway.

title: 38% of AI spiritual advice is sycophancy. The Bible saw this coming.
excerpt: Anthropic studied 1 million Claude conversations. Nearly 4 in 10 spiritual conversations produced sycophancy — the machine telling the user what they wanted to hear.
share_quote: 38% of AI spiritual advice is sycophancy. The Bible called it itching ears 2,000 years ago.

title: 70 people showed up. It took 25 years.
excerpt: The Hartford Institute found median church attendance rose for the first time in decades. The Bible's question is different: why do they gather?
share_quote: 70 people showed up. It took 25 years. The Reformers would ask: what kind of church?

title: 1 in 3 women. The church told her to submit more.
excerpt: The CDC published the numbers this year. The Reformers had a different answer.
share_quote: 1 in 3 women. The church told her to submit more. The Reformers had a different answer.

title: For the first time, young men are more religious than young women.
excerpt: Gallup recorded the reversal this week. Jonathan Edwards would ask: what kind of religion?
share_quote: For the first time, young men are more religious than young women. Jonathan Edwards would ask: what kind of religion?

title: Work is not a curse.
excerpt: The curse is toil. The gift is vocation. And May Day is older than the revolutionaries.
share_quote: Work is not a curse. The curse is toil. The gift is vocation.

title: Your child spends 5 hours a day in worship.
excerpt: Not at church. On a screen. And the algorithm is a better preacher than you.
share_quote: Your child spends 5 hours a day in worship. Not at church. On a screen.

title: 40% of births are now outside marriage.
excerpt: The Census Bureau reported the shift in January. The Reformed tradition has something better than shame to offer: the covenant.
share_quote: 40% of US births are now outside marriage. The Reformers did not moralize. They theologized.

title: 2,362 dead. 217 of them children.
excerpt: The UN reported the toll from Iran this week. The Bible's response to civilian death is not 'God has a plan.' It is 'How long, O Lord?'
share_quote: 2,362 dead in Iran. 217 of them children. The Christian response is not "God has a plan." It is lament.

title: Lost for 500 years.
excerpt: Monks used 42 pages of a 6th-century Bible as scrap paper. Scientists just found them.
share_quote: For 500 years, monks used the New Testament as scrap paper. The text was still there.

title: The mob came with iron rods.
excerpt: Five churches in Kandhamal burned this week. The crime, again, is the name of Christ. Hannah's prayer is a war cry for exactly this.
share_quote: The mob in Odisha came with iron rods. The church meets anyway. The gates of hell will not prevail.

title: Four churches. One week. Zero headlines.
excerpt: Four churches burned in Chhattisgarh while the world scrolled past. Hannah's prayer says the God who permits the fire will also raise the poor from its ashes.
share_quote: Four churches burned in Chhattisgarh. Not a single living stone was touched. The temple Christ builds cannot be burned.

title: The first Christian nation is now prosecuting the church.
excerpt: Armenia adopted Christianity in AD 301. In 2026, its government is banning travel for church leaders and demanding oversight of sermons. Isaiah 33:22 says the LORD is Judge, Lawgiver, and King — not Pashinyan.
share_quote: Armenia was the first Christian nation. Now it bans its pastors from traveling. The Head of the Church does not need a visa.

title: The Vatican found a new partner. It's a machine.
excerpt: The Vatican and Microsoft announced an AI-powered digital archive of St. Peter's Basilica. The press release used 'spiritual' four times. 'Scripture' appeared zero.
share_quote: The Vatican invested millions in AI to digitalize a basilica. The Word of God was already sufficient. It doesn't need an API.

title: Silicon Valley built another god. It's trembling before it.
excerpt: Anthropic released Mythos. The tech press cycled through awe and terror like a liturgy. Romans 1:25 diagnosed this 2,000 years ago: they exchanged the truth of God for a lie and worshiped the creature.
share_quote: Truth is not an output. It is a Person. The machine can generate text. It cannot be the Word.

title: The fact-finding team went to Odisha. They found the same hell as 2008.
excerpt: Beatings. Sexual assault. Burned homes. Police refusing to file FIRs. A Christian fact-finding team documented Kandhamal district 18 years after the pogrom. The pattern is unchanged.
share_quote: The church in Odisha has been conquering for 18 years without an army. The weapons: the blood of the Lamb, the word of their testimony.

title: Trump met the Pope. Acts 5:29 was the only readout that mattered.
excerpt: The handshake was photographed. The readout said 'cordial.' But the president who deports immigrants and the pope who defends them represent two incompatible theologies of power. Neither is the head of the church.
share_quote: The church does not have a foreign policy. It has a confession. Both presidents and popes breathe borrowed air.

title: They announced peace in Iran. Jeremiah 6:14 has the rebuttal.
excerpt: The ceasefire was signed. The diplomats said 'peace.' But the rubble in Tehran is still there. The 217 dead children are still dead. A ceasefire is a legal instrument — not a resurrection.
share_quote: A ceasefire is a legal instrument. It is not a resurrection. The bombs have stopped. The dead are still dead.

title: They stormed the Mass with knives. The police arrested the Catholics.
excerpt: A mob armed with knives interrupted a Sunday Mass in Rajasthan. The police filed charges — against the Catholics. Acts 4:19-20 was written for this moment.
share_quote: They stormed the Mass with knives. The police arrested the Catholics. The Judge of all the earth will not be fooled by an FIR.

title: Ontario wanted to teach 16-year-olds how to die. The backlash paused it.
excerpt: Ontario proposed MAID curriculum modules for high school students — state-facilitated death taught like driver's education. You are not your own. You were bought with a price.
share_quote: You are not your own. You were bought with a price. The state does not get to teach teenagers otherwise.

title: India proposed a Christian Welfare Board. The velvet is a chain.
excerpt: The bill promises to coordinate, streamline, and facilitate Christian services. But the state that burns churches and then offers to register them is not serving the church. It is managing it.
share_quote: The state that burns churches and then offers to register them is not serving the church. It is managing it.

title: $42 million for costumes while Christians sat in an Indian jail.
excerpt: The Met Gala raised $42M for the Costume Institute. The same week, Catholics were arrested in Rajasthan and Christians were beaten in Odisha. The blessed are not on the red carpet.
share_quote: The Met Gala raised $42M while Christians in Rajasthan were arrested for attending Mass. The blessed are not on the red carpet.

title: Wyoming banned abortion. Not because life is sacred — because the state needs workers.
excerpt: Wyoming's new abortion law frames children as a state resource to combat demographic decline. The imago Dei is not a labor force projection. A child's value doesn't come from GDP.
share_quote: Wyoming banned abortion — not because life is sacred, but because the state needs workers. The imago Dei is not a demographic projection.

title: R.F. Kuang wrote a novel with an Israeli character. The boycott came before anyone read it.
excerpt: The author of Babel faces a review-bombing campaign over a fictional character's nationality. The same literary world that defended Rushdie is silent. Exodus 20:16 applies to review-bombers too.
share_quote: They review-bombed a book they never read because a fictional character is Israeli. That is not justice. That is tribalism in moral drag.

title: Samsung bet a trillion dollars on chips. Colossians 1:17 holds the silicon together.
excerpt: Samsung is investing $1 trillion. Nvidia and Corning are building a $2.7 billion factory. Impressive numbers. Not saving numbers. The silicon holds together because Christ holds it together.
share_quote: $1 trillion is an impressive number. It is not a saving number. The silicon holds together because Christ holds it together.
